page: Amazon Web Services i8ge.24xlarge is a storage-optimized instance powered by the AWS Graviton Neoverse-V2 processor, utilizing the ARM64 architecture. The server features 96 dedicated physical cores operating at 2.8 GHz with a single thread per core, eliminating hyperthreading overhead. It is equipped with 768.0 GB of system memory, providing 8.0 GB of RAM per core. For local storage, the instance includes 60,000 GB of NVMe SSD capacity. Network connectivity is supported by a baseline bandwidth of 150 Gbps, managed via the AWS Nitro hypervisor.

In benchmark evaluations, the i8ge.24xlarge demonstrates top-tier performance across multiple multi-threaded and memory-intensive workloads. Multi-core CPU performance via stress-ng and multi-threaded bzip3 decompression both rank in the top 10% of tested instances. The memory subsystem shows high throughput, with read-only and write-only large block memory bandwidth tests reaching top-tier classifications. Database operations, specifically Redis SET operations and PassMark database benchmarks, also place in the top 10%, alongside static web serving throughput. Single-core performance, measured by stress-ng and Geekbench, falls into the average to strong categories, indicating that the instance's strength lies in parallelized, multi-core execution.

Given its massive 60,000 GB NVMe SSD storage capacity and high memory-to-core ratio, the i8ge.24xlarge offers high resource density for storage-intensive and database workloads. The use of custom AWS Graviton ARM64 processors provides a cost-efficient alternative to traditional x86 architectures for highly parallelized tasks. This server is suited for large-scale databases, high-throughput web serving, software compilation, and data-intensive applications requiring low-latency local storage and high network bandwidth.
description: Amazon Web Services i8ge.24xlarge is a storage-optimized instance built on the ARM64 architecture and powered by 96 dedicated AWS Graviton Neoverse-V2 physical cores running at 2.8 GHz. It features 768.0 GB of RAM, 60,000 GB of local NVMe SSD storage, and a 150 Gbps baseline network bandwidth managed by the Nitro hypervisor. In benchmark testing, the instance delivers top-tier multi-core CPU performance, exceptional memory bandwidth, and high database throughput, with Redis SET operations and static web serving placing in the top 10%. While single-core performance is moderate, its massive parallel processing capabilities and dense local storage make it highly cost-efficient for data-intensive workloads. The i8ge.24xlarge is suited for large-scale databases, high-throughput web serving, and parallelized data processing tasks.
